reasoning: Elon Musk has a history of heavily missing ambitious FSD timelines (e.g., promising 1 million robotaxis by 2020). The market creator's updates in June 2025 clarifying that observers and safety backups are not allowed and pushing back against loopholes (Smart Summon, tiny distances) strongly implies that Tesla's mid-2025 service launch in Austin involved human monitoring or tester rides. Achieving true, completely uncrewed, un-observed L4 autonomy capable of doing 10,000 genuine non-tester passenger trips safely within the remaining six months of 2025 relies on an unprecedented leap in absolute reliability for their vision-only end-to-end system. I therefore assign a low probability of 7% that Tesla successfully achieved this by the end of 2025.
